frameworks.name: brain signal variability (BOLD variability)
frameworks.one_line: trial-to-trial fluctuations in BOLD as a neural index of inhibitory function

frameworks.name: response inhibition / stop-signal
frameworks.one_line: stop-signal task indexes inhibitory control via stop-signal latency

frameworks.name: developmental neuroscience
frameworks.one_line: compares children and adults to chart maturation of inhibition circuitry

core_question: How do intra-individual variability in stopping performance and underlying BOLD variability change from childhood to adulthood?
key_assumptions: intra-individual variability is informative beyond mean performance
BOLD variability indexes functional state of an inhibition network
stop-signal latency reflects an inhibitory process
key_findings: mean stopping performance did not differ between children and adults
stop-signal latencies were more variable in children
BOLD variability during successful stopping was higher in adults, notably in bilateral thalamus and the inhibition network
BOLD variability was associated with behavioral stopping variability in adults
interpretations: behavioral variability in inhibition decreases while neural variability increases across development
increasing neural variability may support more stable inhibitory control
